user_story_groups.story: As the main operator, I can keep running normal Codex locally while seeing exactly how a future Symphony worker would pass the same envelope contract.
evidence: symphony-template-prepare.json returns status=ready through the same helper
reference docs state Codexter is not a separate CLI or scheduler

user_story_groups.story: As an integration builder, I know what Symphony must own and what Codexter must return without guessing from old chat context.
evidence: references/symphony.md includes a responsibility split for polling, claims, workspaces, Codex launch, proof, and tracker writes

user_story_groups.story: As a team user later using Linear/Symphony, I can trust Codexter will not overclaim live Symphony support before the actual backend exists.
evidence: reference states this is a shim/template, not a live Symphony backend
template uses computeTarget=local_shared because Symphony already chose the workspace

user_story_groups.story: As a maintainer, I can verify the template stays valid and the proof path remains machine-readable.
evidence: bin/test_codexter_invocation.py covers preparing the Symphony envelope template from file
template and smoke output are valid JSON

risks.risk: The shim may be mistaken for a live Symphony backend.
risks.reason: The README, SKILL, and reference all say Symphony owns scheduler/runtime mechanics and this ticket ships only a template plus smoke proof.

risks.risk: Real Symphony app-server details can drift.
risks.reason: The shim avoids protocol implementation and treats Symphony/Codex app-server launch as the external runner's source-of-truth responsibility.

commands: python3 - <<'PY' ... feature registry status-aware validation ... PY
